response.justification: The role requires native-level Japanese, strong C for UNIX development, Linux kernel source modification, and embedded LAN-switch security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 networking). The candidate is a strong full-stack/web engineer (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) with only dated, peripheral C/C++ exposure (LabWindows/CVI, a sound server) and no demonstrated networking, switch firmware, kernel-level, or security-protocol experience. Critically, no Japanese proficiency is listed. The mismatch on the hard requirements makes this candidate unsuitable.
strengths: Broad, long software engineering track record across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded work
Some hands-on C/C++ experience (NI LabWindows/CVI, C++ sound server) and Linux familiarity
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, DevOps tooling, and open-source contribution
concerns: No Japanese language ability listed, despite native-level Japanese being a hard requirement
No embedded networking, LAN-switch, L2/L3, or security-protocol (MACsec, 802.1X) experience
C and Linux-kernel modification skills are minimal and dated relative to the core C/UNIX requirement
